clusters.title: Major Russian missile and drone attacks on Ukraine kill several, wound dozens
clusters.neutral_headline: Russian Attacks Kill People In Ukraine

items.title: Major Russian missile and drone attacks on Ukraine kill several, wound dozens
items.standfirst: Russian missile and drone strikes killed several people and wounded dozens across Ukraine, authorities said, days after President Zelenskyy warned Moscow was preparing a "massive new strike."
items.content: Russian missile and drone strikes killed several people and wounded dozens across Ukraine, authorities said, days after President Zelenskyy warned Moscow was preparing a "massive new strike."

items.title: At least nine people killed, dozens wounded in Russian attacks on Ukraine
items.standfirst: Russia launches 656 drones and 73 missiles at Ukraine overnight, according to Ukraine's air force.
items.content: Russia launches 656 drones and 73 missiles at Ukraine overnight, according to Ukraine's air force.

items.title: Wave of Russian strikes on Ukrainian cities kill at least 11
items.standfirst: Dozens injured and people trapped under rubble of fallen buildings after attacks on Kyiv, Dnipro and KharkivRussian air raids on major Ukrainian centres including Kyiv, Dnipro and Kharkiv have killed at least 11 people, injured dozens and left others trapped.Russia launched 73 missiles and 656 drones at Ukraine, according to the Ukrainian air force, with the main targets including Kyiv, the central city of Dnipro and the eastern cities of Poltava, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia. Ukrainian air defences destroyed or suppressed 40 missiles and 602 drones. Continue reading...
items.content: Dozens injured and people trapped under rubble of fallen buildings after attacks on Kyiv, Dnipro and KharkivRussian air raids on major Ukrainian centres including Kyiv, Dnipro and Kharkiv have killed at least 11 people, injured dozens and left others trapped.Russia launched 73 missiles and 656 drones at Ukraine, according to the Ukrainian air force, with the main targets including Kyiv, the central city of Dnipro and the eastern cities of Poltava, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia. Ukrainian air defences destroyed or suppressed 40 missiles and 602 drones. Continue reading...

items.title: Massive Russian attack on cities across Ukraine kills at least 10 people
items.standfirst: Rescuers are racing to find people feared trapped beneath rubble in Kyiv after the overnight missile and drone attack.
items.content: Rescuers are racing to find people feared trapped beneath rubble in Kyiv after the overnight missile and drone attack.

items.title: Russian attack on Ukraine kills at least 11 and traps others in damaged buildings
items.standfirst: Russia attacked Ukraine overnight Tuesday, killing at least 11 people and trapping others, authorities said. Residential buildings and other civilian infrastructure were damaged in eight of Kyiv's districts.
items.content: Russia attacked Ukraine overnight Tuesday, killing at least 11 people and trapping others, authorities said. Residential buildings and other civilian infrastructure were damaged in eight of Kyiv's districts.(Image credit: Efrem Lukatsky)

items.title: Russian attacks kill several people and wound more than 100 across Ukraine
items.standfirst: Russian air strikes hit several major Ukrainian cities early on Tuesday, killing at least 11 people and wounding more than 100, authorities said. Kyiv, Dnipro and Kharkiv were among the hardest hit, with residential buildings damaged and thousands of residents sheltering underground amid ongoing air raid alerts.
items.content: Russian air strikes hit several major Ukrainian cities early on Tuesday, killing at least 11 people and wounding more than 100, authorities said. Kyiv, Dnipro and Kharkiv were among the hardest hit, with residential buildings damaged and thousands of residents sheltering underground amid ongoing air raid alerts.

ai_summary: Russian missile and drone strikes kill several people
Dozens wounded across Ukraine in overnight attacks
Ukraine's air force reports 656 drones and 73 missiles launched
Air defenses destroyed or suppressed 40 missiles and 602 drones
Residential buildings damaged in eight Kyiv districts

items.title: Alphabet Will Raise $80 Billion to Fund AI Spending
items.standfirst: Google parent Alphabet will raise $80 billion in equity capital to pay for ambitious artificial intelligence spending plans. Mandeep Singh of Bloomberg Intelligence has more. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: Google parent Alphabet will raise $80 billion in equity capital to pay for ambitious artificial intelligence spending plans. Mandeep Singh of Bloomberg Intelligence has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Alphabet to Raise $80 Billion in Equity for AI Spending
items.standfirst: Google parent Alphabet Inc. is raising $80 billion through a package of equity offerings, including an investment deal with Berkshire Hathaway Inc., as the company races to fund its ambitious artificial intelligence spending plans.
items.content: Google parent Alphabet Inc. is raising $80 billion through a package of equity offerings, including an investment deal with Berkshire Hathaway Inc., as the company races to fund its ambitious artificial intelligence spending plans.

clusters.title: Denmark's Mette Frederiksen to form government after months of negotiations

items.title: Denmark's Mette Frederiksen to form government after months of negotiations
items.standfirst: The leader of Denmark's Social Democratic Party is forming a centre-left coalition minority government, giving her a third term as prime minister.
items.content: The leader of Denmark's Social Democratic Party is forming a centre-left coalition minority government, giving her a third term as prime minister.

items.title: Danish PM Frederiksen secures third term with new coalition government
items.standfirst: Denmark’s Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen announced on Monday that she has formed a new left-leaning minority coalition after months of negotiations following inconclusive elections in March. The agreement secures her third term in office, bringing together four parties that hold a slim parliamentary majority.
items.content: Denmark’s Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen announced on Monday that she has formed a new left-leaning minority coalition after months of negotiations following inconclusive elections in March. The agreement secures her third term in office, bringing together four parties that hold a slim parliamentary majority.

items.title: Denmark’s Mette Frederiksen agrees centre-left coalition after months of uncertainty
items.standfirst: Social Democratic leader secures third consecutive term as prime minister amid crisis in ties with US over GreenlandDenmark’s Social Democratic leader, Mette Frederiksen, said on Monday she had agreed to form a centre-left coalition government, maintaining her grip on power amid a crisis in ties with Donald Trump over Greenland.The deal to form a minority cabinet gives Frederiksen a third consecutive term as prime minister, ending months of uncertainty after a March election in which 12 parties won seats in the Danish parliament. Continue reading...
items.content: Social Democratic leader secures third consecutive term as prime minister amid crisis in ties with US over GreenlandDenmark’s Social Democratic leader, Mette Frederiksen, said on Monday she had agreed to form a centre-left coalition government, maintaining her grip on power amid a crisis in ties with Donald Trump over Greenland.The deal to form a minority cabinet gives Frederiksen a third consecutive term as prime minister, ending months of uncertainty after a March election in which 12 parties won seats in the Danish parliament. Continue reading...

ai_summary: Mette Frederiksen to form centre-left coalition government
Frederiksen secures third term as prime minister of Denmark
Agreement brings together four parties with slim majority
Centre-left coalition government formed after months of talks
Frederiksen maintains grip on power in Denmark

clusters.title: Trump reportedly mulling retreat from $1.8bn ‘anti-weaponization’ fund

items.title: Trump reportedly mulling retreat from $1.8bn ‘anti-weaponization’ fund
items.standfirst: Democrats decry president’s ‘most brazen act of self-dealing yet’ and vow to challenge fund in CongressDonald Trump is reconsidering whether to keep pressing for a $1.8bn fund to compensate his allies, a person familiar with his thinking said on Monday, as the justice department paused the program to comply with a court order.Trump’s “anti-weaponization” fund has faced legal setbacks since it was announced two weeks ago. The idea has also faced a mounting political backlash from Republicans concerned by a lack of oversight and the possibility of payouts to participants in the January 6 2021 riot at the US Capitol. Continue reading...
items.content: Democrats decry president’s ‘most brazen act of self-dealing yet’ and vow to challenge fund in CongressDonald Trump is reconsidering whether to keep pressing for a $1.8bn fund to compensate his allies, a person familiar with his thinking said on Monday, as the justice department paused the program to comply with a court order.Trump’s “anti-weaponization” fund has faced legal setbacks since it was announced two weeks ago. The idea has also faced a mounting political backlash from Republicans concerned by a lack of oversight and the possibility of payouts to participants in the January 6 2021 riot at the US Capitol. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump reconsiders $1.8 billion ally compensation fund amid Republican backlash
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump was said to be reconsidering a controversial $1.8 billion compensation fund on Monday, created as part of a settlement with the IRS, amid legal challenges and growing opposition from Republicans. The Justice Department has paused implementation following a court order, raising doubts about the fund's future.
items.content: US President Donald Trump was said to be reconsidering a controversial $1.8 billion compensation fund on Monday, created as part of a settlement with the IRS, amid legal challenges and growing opposition from Republicans. The Justice Department has paused implementation following a court order, raising doubts about the fund's future.

ai_summary: Trump reconsidering $1.8 billion ally compensation fund
Fund faces legal challenges and Republican backlash
Justice Department pauses implementation of fund
Trump's "anti-weaponization" fund faces opposition
Fund's future uncertain amid legal and political challenges

clusters.title: Williams, 44, to return to action in Queen's doubles

items.title: Williams, 44, to return to action in Queen's doubles
items.standfirst: Serena Williams will return to competitive tennis for the first time in nearly four years when she plays in the women's doubles at Queen's.
items.content: Serena Williams will return to competitive tennis for the first time in nearly four years when she plays in the women's doubles at Queen's.

items.title: Serena Williams returns to tennis, announcing 'next chapter'
items.standfirst: A tennis legend at 44, Serena Williams is returning to pro tennis this month, announcing plans to play in the HSBC Championships in London. Williams, a mother of two, retired nearly four years ago.
items.content: A tennis legend at 44, Serena Williams is returning to pro tennis this month, announcing plans to play in the HSBC Championships in London. Williams, a mother of two, retired nearly four years ago.(Image credit: Angela Weiss)

items.title: French navy seizes Russia-linked oil tanker in high seas, Macron says
items.standfirst: The French Navy on Sunday boarded an oil tanker, named the Tagor, which was subject to international sanctions and sailing from Russia, French President Emmanuel Macron wrote on X.
items.content: The French Navy on Sunday boarded an oil tanker, named the Tagor, which was subject to international sanctions and sailing from Russia, French President Emmanuel Macron wrote on X.
